zh.York, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom: 英國英格蘭越可爾約克
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William de Ros of Helmsley於1192年出生在英國越可爾爾姆斯利時，他的父親Sir Robert de Ros I22歲，母親Isabel of Scotland28歲。他於1225年與Lucy Fitzpeter在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他於1235年被列為member of parliament。他去世於1264年在英國越可爾享年72歲，埋葬在英國越可爾吉可哈姆。

zh-hans.York, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom: 英国英格兰越可爾約克
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William de Ros of Helmsley于1192年出生在英国越可爾爾姆斯利时，他的父亲Sir Robert de Ros I22岁，母亲Isabel of Scotland28岁。他于1225年与Lucy Fitzpeter在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他于1235年被列为member of parliament。他去世于1264年在英国越可爾享年72岁，埋葬在英国越可爾吉可哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de Ros of Helmsley was born in 1192, in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir Robert de Ros I, was 22 and his mother, Isabel of Scotland, was 28. He married Lucy Fitzpeter in 1225, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. In 1235, his occupation is listed as member of parliament. He died in 1264, in Yorkshire, England, at the age of 72, and was buried in Kirkham, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William de Ros of Helmsley est né en 1192, à Helmsley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert de Ros I, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Isabel of Scotland, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Lucy Fitzpeter en 1225, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 3 filles. En 1235, sa profession de son indiquée est member of parliament. Il est décédé en 1264, à Everwyck, Angleterre, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterré à Kirkham, Everwyck, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William de Ros of Helmsley 1192 in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Robert de Ros I 22 und seine Mutter Isabel of Scotland 28. Er hat 1225 in Yorkshire, England Lucy Fitzpeter geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 3 Töchter. 1235 ist sein Beruf als member of parliament aufgeführt. Er starb 1264 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Yorkshire, England und wurde in Kirkham, Yorkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de Ros of Helmsley è nato il nel 1192 nel Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Robert de Ros I, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Isabel of Scotland, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Lucy Fitzpeter nel 1225, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 3 figlie. Nel 1235, la sua occupazione è indicata come member of parliament. Morì nel 1264, nel Yorkshire, England, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolto a Kirkham, Yorkshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de Ros of Helmsley(이)가 1192년 영국 요르크실르 헬슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert de Ros I(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel of Scotland(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1225년 영국 요르크실르에서 Lucy Fitzpeter(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1235년, 그의 직업은 member of parliament이었다. 그는 1264년 영국 요르크실르에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 커크함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de Ros of Helmsley nasceu em 1192, em Helmsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert de Ros I, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Isabel of Scotland, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Lucy Fitzpeter em 1225, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Em 1235, sua profissão aparece como member of parliament. Ele faleceu em 1264, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, com 72 anos, e foi sepultado em Kirkham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William de Ros of Helmsley родился в 1192 г. в местности хелмсли, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Robert de Ros I было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel of Scotland, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Lucy Fitzpeter в 1225 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. В 1235 году он работал по профессии member of parliament. Он умер в 1264 г. в местности еркшир, Англия в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности кирхам, еркшир, Англия.
